text: World Cancer Research Fund is dedicated to examining how diet, body weight and physical activity affect your risk of developing and surviving cancer. Last month, 41 of our incredible supporters ran through the streets of London to fundraise for vital research into cancer prevention and survival.

Building research where it matters: lessons from studying breast cancer in Sub-Saharan Africa Prof Reginald Annan makes the case for African-led research – and shares what building it from the ground up actually looks like. Rachael Hutson, CEO at World Cancer Research Fund, reflects on the London Marathon 2026 and shares other ways to support our life-saving research.

Ultra-processed foods: reflections on what we know and what is coming As consumption of ultra-processed foods rises globally, so do concerns about their health impacts. We unpack what UPFs are, examine emerging evidence on cancer risk, and highlight why further research is critical. Every step our runners, walkers and skydivers tells a story.

Take our test today Free online cookery classes for people affected by cancer Our prescription to help you reduce your risk of cancer Our flagship research work has transformed the field of cancer prevention We fund innovative and original research globally We want cancer prevention to be a top priority for governments Lace up your trainers and raise money for cancer prevention Choose your numbers and you could soon be on a winning streak with our game Remember someone special with a tribute in their name While society continues to search for a cure, our prevention and survival work helps people to live longer, healthier lives – free from the devastating effects of cancer.
